response:
Here are comprehensive unit tests for the `rgb_to_hex` function using pytest style, covering happy path, edge cases, and error cases:

```python
import pytest
from your_module import rgb_to_hex  # Replace 'your_module' with the actual module name

class TestRgbToHex:
    """Test cases for rgb_to_hex function."""

    # Happy path tests
    @pytest.mark.parametrize("r, g, b, expected", [
        (0, 0, 0, "#000000"),        # Black
        (255, 255, 255, "#ffffff"),  # White
        (255, 0, 0, "#ff0000"),      # Red
        (0, 255, 0, "#00ff00"),      # Green
        (0, 0, 255, "#0000ff"),      # Blue
        (16, 32, 64, "#102040"),     # Various values
        (1, 15, 255, "#010fff"),     # Single digit values
        (255, 255, 254, "#fffffe"),  # Almost white
    ])
    def test_happy_path(self, r, g, b, expected):
        """Test valid RGB values return correct hex strings."""
        assert rgb_to_hex(r, g, b) == expected

    # Edge cases
    @pytest.mark.parametrize("r, g, b, expected", [
        (0, 0, 0, "#000000"),        # Minimum values
        (255, 255, 255, "#ffffff"),  # Maximum values
        (16, 16, 16, "#101010"),     # Same values
        (128, 128, 128, "#808080"),  # Gray
    ])
    def test_edge_cases(self, r, g, b, expected):
        """Test edge case RGB values."""
        assert rgb_to_hex(r, g, b) == expected

    # Error cases
    @pytest.mark.parametrize("r, g, b", [
        (-1, 0, 0),                  # Negative red
        (0, -1, 0),                  # Negative green
        (0, 0, -1),                  # Negative blue
        (256, 0, 0),                 # Red too high
        (0, 256, 0),                 # Green too high
        (0, 0, 256),                 # Blue too high
        (300, 100, 50),              # All values out of range
        (-10, -20, -30),             # All negative
    ])
    def test_invalid_values_raise_error(self, r, g, b):
        """Test that invalid RGB values raise ValueError."""
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="RGB values must be 0-255"):
            rgb_to_hex(r, g, b)

    # Type error cases
    @pytest.mark.parametrize("r, g, b", [
        ("255", 0, 0),               # String instead of int
        (0, [0], 0),                 # List instead of int
        (0, 0, None),                # None instead of int
        (0.5, 0, 0),                 # Float instead of int
    ])
    def test_non_integer_values_raise_error(self, r, g, b):
        """Test that non-integer values raise TypeError."""
        with pytest.raises(TypeError):
            rgb_to_hex(r, g, b)

    # Test case sensitivity (should always be lowercase)
    def test_hex_output_is_lowercase(self):
        """Test that hex output is always lowercase."""
        assert rgb_to_hex(255, 1, 15) == "#ff010f"
        assert rgb_to_hex(10, 20, 30) == "#0a141e"

    # Test zero padding
    def test_single_digit_values_are_zero_padded(self):
        """Test that single digit values are properly zero-padded."""
        assert rgb_to_hex(1, 1, 1) == "#01
